identity.title: Metal Coating, Engraving (except Jewelry and Silverware), and Allied Services to Manufacturers

lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market job shops repeatedly enameling, lacquering, varnishing, painting, powder coating, hot-dip galvanizing, engraving, etching, or otherwise surfacing customer-owned metal products for manufacturers and industrial customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive in-house finishing lines, electroplating and anodizing classified in 332813, jewelry and printing-plate engraving, firms coating only their own fabricated products, consumer decorative shops, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring job-shop and production-program revenue billed per piece, square foot, pound, batch, line hour, or quoted program, with charges for pretreatment, masking, materials, color or finish changes, inspection, packaging, and expedite service.

a.bridge: BLS reports that metal and plastic machine-worker employment is projected to decline 7% from 2024 to 2034 and that machine operators monitor machinery and products, while OSHA describes finishing processes that require ventilation, immersion-tank controls, and physical handling. I map quoting, drawing and specification review, order entry, color and material planning, line scheduling, certificate and inspection records, maintenance knowledge, and customer status work to AI exposure, while treating racking, masking, preparation, spraying, tank handling, inspection, packaging, cleaning, and maintenance as physical. The occupation proxy spans many manufacturing processes and may overstate AI-addressable work in labor-intensive masking shops.
a.rationale: Surface-finishing shops contain meaningful information work around quotes, routings, colors, schedules, quality, compliance, and customer communication, but a large share of wage-weighted effort remains physical preparation, handling, application, inspection, and rework.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted task and time studies across powder-coating, galvanizing, liquid-coating, and engraving shops, including installed ERP and line automation, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No public six-digit occupation matrix isolates 332812.
The broad process mix produces large dispersion: automated conveyor powder lines differ from manual masking and custom engraving shops.

rho.rationale: AI can be embedded in quoting, schedule optimization, specification review, visual-quality triage, documentation, and maintenance within five years. Dirty data, old shop systems, custom part presentation, color and finish liability, customer approvals, worker adoption, and environmental controls limit the implemented share.
rho.change_evidence: Audited deployments at independent finishers measuring quote labor, schedule performance, inspection false positives, rework, maintenance downtime, record labor, and accepted recommendations would materially change the range.
caveats: This is bounded judgment, not an observed adoption rate.
Computer vision may improve faster than integration with legacy conveyor, oven, and pretreatment controls.

e.rationale: The industry definition explicitly centers coating, engraving, and surfacing services for the trade or to manufacturers, closely matching repeat outsourced service. Ineligibility remains for one-off decorative work, captive-like customer concentration, mixed businesses dominated by fabrication, or firms without transferable standalone operations.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm census of the injected LMM population using external-customer revenue, repeat-order share, ownership, captive status, fabrication mix, and customer concentration would directly establish eligibility.
caveats: Industry classification does not prove recurrence, economic band, or transferability.
Some establishments may combine qualifying finishing with nonqualifying fabrication or retail activity.

s5.bridge: Census reports that 51% of responding US employer-business owners were age 55 or older in 2018. I convert broad owner-aging pressure into a five-year qualifying-control-transfer probability and reduce it for family transfers, closures, internal reorganizations, minority recaps, environmental liabilities, and owner-dependent customer relationships. The all-industry statistic may understate aging in mature industrial job shops but does not measure firm sales.
s5.rationale: The large independent job-shop population plausibly creates succession opportunities, although environmental records, customer concentration, facility leases, and owner relationships determine whether the operating company transfers cleanly.
s5.change_evidence: Owner-age and succession interviews across commercial finishers, plus five-year GF Data, Axial, PitchBook, and Capital IQ transactions coded for control, facility continuity, and standalone operations, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The proxy is not industry- or size-specific and measures owners rather than transactions.
Cooperative, corporate, and sponsor-owned firms have no individual-owner retirement trigger.

q.rationale: Approved finishes, color matching, pretreatment know-how, freight, quality history, rapid turnaround, and line fit create switching friction. Competitive local quoting, per-piece pricing, customer productivity requests, alternate shops, material pass-through, and annual sourcing reviews share savings over five years.
q.change_evidence: A representative contract, quote, and invoice sample showing material escalation, open-book terms, cost-down clauses, annual review cadence, minimum lots, qualification costs, expedite fees, win rates, and churn would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No public customer-contract sample was available.
Capture is stronger for qualified technical finishes and weaker for standard powder colors on easily shipped parts.

d5.bridge: Federal Reserve industrial production for broader NAICS 3328 fell from 108.46 in 2021 Q3 to 86.73 in 2024 Q4 and recovered to 91.44 by 2026 Q1; BLS broader 33281 sectoral output fell 1.7% in 2023 and 3.7% in 2024. These series include heat treating, electroplating, engraving, and allied services as well as coating, so they are imperfect proxies for 332812. The broad mix can understate healthier local niches or overstate weak end markets.
d5.rationale: Manufacturers continue to require protective, decorative, and functional finishes across diverse products, but service volume tracks cyclical metal fabrication and customer reshoring or offshoring. Recent weakness followed by partial recovery supports a roughly flat five-year base and wide range.
d5.change_evidence: Six-digit real shipment data, customer-industry revenue cohorts, production-program backlogs, line utilization, and five-year forecasts from machinery, construction, vehicle, aerospace, and consumer-product customers would materially change the range.
caveats: Available real-output series are broader than 332812.
Coating demand varies with part mix, surface area, finish specification, and domestic manufacturing location, not just unit output.

o.rationale: Remaining service quantity requires physical pretreatment, application, curing or galvanizing, inspection, environmental control, and accountable release. Software can automate ordering and process decisions but cannot place a durable finish on the part; some customers may insource automated coating lines or redesign away the finish.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of customer insourcing, prefinished material substitution, additive manufacturing with finished surfaces, or autonomous coating cells that eliminate external accountable processors would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The accountable operator can consolidate into larger networks even if the physical service remains necessary.
Labor automation is represented in a and rho rather than deducted again here.

narrative.executive_view: Metal coating and engraving is a large, naturally outsourced job-shop industry with recurring manufacturer programs. AI can improve quoting, specification review, scheduling, quality records, visual inspection, and maintenance, while freight, qualification, finish consistency, and turnaround support some retention; physical processing and recent cyclical weakness limit the upside.
narrative.ai_impact: Near-term uses include drawing and specification parsing, automated quotes and routings, color and material planning, line scheduling, certificate preparation, visual defect triage, maintenance knowledge, and customer updates. Racking, masking, preparation, spraying, dipping, curing, galvanizing, inspection, cleaning, and rework remain physical.
narrative.value_capture: Approved processes, color consistency, local logistics, line fit, quality history, and fast turnaround create switching costs. Standard jobs remain competitively quoted, and material escalation plus customer productivity demands transmit part of the gain.
narrative.firm_availability: The definition's service-to-manufacturers orientation means most standalone firms fit the lens, and the estimated firm population is large. Succession should create supply, but environmental diligence, customer concentration, and owner-dependent sales can impair transferability.
narrative.demand_durability: Protective and decorative finishes remain physically necessary across many manufactured products. Broader industry output declined through 2024 before a partial recovery, so a flat base with cyclical downside is more defensible than strong structural growth.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Key risks are manufacturing cycles, customer concentration, environmental and worker-safety liabilities, coating chemistry changes, energy and material costs, reject and rework exposure, owner dependence, customer insourcing, and unavailable six-digit task and real-demand data.
narrative.principal_driver: A broad recurring outsourced-service base benefits from AI-addressable order, schedule, quality, and compliance workflows.
narrative.principal_weakness: Physical shop labor, environmental liabilities, and competitive per-part pricing constrain implemented and retained gains.

sources.title: NAICS definition: 332812 Metal Coating, Engraving, and Allied Services to Manufacturers
sources.supports: Defines enameling, lacquering, varnishing, hot-dip galvanizing, engraving, etching, powder coating, painting, and other surfacing services for the trade.

sources.title: Metal and Plastic Machine Workers
sources.supports: Describes machine operators as monitoring equipment and products and reports a projected 7% decline in aggregate metal and plastic machine-worker employment from 2024 to 2034.

sources.title: OSHA 1926.57 Ventilation
sources.supports: Describes immersion, spray finishing, galvanizing, surface coating, draining, drying, ventilation, and employee exposure controls for finishing operations.

sources.title: Occupational projections and worker characteristics
sources.supports: Reports projected declines for multiple metal-processing occupations, including a 13.6% decline for plating-machine operators, as a directional automation proxy.

sources.title: Miscellaneous Metal Parts and Products Surface Coating NESHAP
sources.supports: Describes HAP standards for miscellaneous metal-part coating and identifies regulated organic pollutants such as xylenes, toluene, ketones, glycol ethers, styrene, and ethyl benzene.

sources.title: Technical Support Document for Miscellaneous Metal Parts and Products Surface Coating NESHAP
sources.supports: Describes diverse coated products, coating application, pretreatment, curing, environmental controls, monitoring, recordkeeping, and reporting burdens.

sources.title: Business Owners' Ages: Over Half of U.S. Business Owners Were Age 55 and Over
sources.supports: Reports that 51% of responding owners of employer businesses were age 55 or older in the 2019 ABS, data year 2018.

sources.title: Industrial Production: Coating, Engraving, Heat Treating, and Allied Activities
sources.supports: Provides broader NAICS 3328 quarterly real-output values, including decline through 2024 and partial recovery through 2026 Q1.

sources.title: Sectoral Output for NAICS 33281
sources.supports: Reports annual sectoral-output changes for broader NAICS 33281, including declines of 1.7% in 2023 and 3.7% in 2024.

sources.title: EPA surface-coating residual risk and technology review proposal
sources.supports: Lists the broad manufactured-product end markets using miscellaneous metal surface coating and estimates 368 facilities subject to the relevant NESHAP.
